title: Mustardy Jamaican Beetroot Empanadas
description: A comforting, deeply savory hangover cure featuring earthy cooked beetroot spiced with fiery Scotch bonnet and sharp Jamaican mustard, all encased in a super-flaky pastry and deep-fried to golden perfection.

ingredients.name: curry powder
ingredients.notes: for color and flavor in the dough

instructions: Whisk the flour, curry powder, and a pinch of salt together in a large bowl.
Cut the cold vegetable shortening into the flour mixture using a pastry cutter until it resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add the ice-cold water, mixing gently until a stiff dough forms, then wrap it in plastic and chill for 30 minutes.
In a separate bowl, combine the diced beetroot, crushed mustard seeds, prepared mustard, Scotch bonnet, and scallions to make the filling.
Roll out the chilled pastry dough on a floured surface to an eighth-inch thickness and cut out four-inch circles.
Place two tablespoons of the beetroot filling onto one half of each circle.
Fold the dough over the filling to create a half-moon shape and press the edges firmly with a fork to seal.
Heat the vegetable oil in a deep pot or wok to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
Fry the empanadas in batches of two or three for about five minutes until they are crispy and golden brown.
Drain the finished empanadas on paper towels and serve them hot.
